frameworks.name: multivoxel pattern analysis
frameworks.one_line: local spatial activity patterns indexing stimulus-driven shared cortical responses

frameworks.name: intersubject correlation
frameworks.one_line: extended from temporal-only ISC to spatial-pattern correlation across subjects

core_question: Are local meso-scale spatial patterns of cortical activity shared across subjects during naturalistic viewing, and how do they relate to shared temporal responses?
key_assumptions: minimum interpolation (no functional alignment) preserves native spatial pattern structure
naturalistic nonsemantic stimulation drives baseline shared responses
spatial and temporal components of shared response are dissociable
key_findings: meso-scale spatial patterns are shared nonuniformly across visual cortex
shared spatial patterns carry information distinct from the shared temporal response
spatial patterns are modulated by pattern size
spatial patterns are more sensitive to 3D vs 2D contrast than temporal signals are
interpretations: stimulus-driven local spatial structure is a separate, nonuniform channel of shared cortical response
spatial pattern correlations offer added sensitivity over temporal ISC for certain stimulus features (e.g., depth)
